headline: Chevron (CVX) Signs 20 Year Microsoft Deal To Power Texas AI Data Center
summary: Chevron (NYSE:CVX) has entered a 20 year agreement to supply natural gas power to a major Microsoft AI data center in West Texas. The project, known as Project Kilby, involves Chevron, Microsoft and energy infrastructure firm Joulent. The deal uses Chevron's resource position in the Permian Basin to provide electricity tailored to AI data center power needs. For readers tracking Chevron, this move extends the company beyond its traditional upstream and downstream oil and gas focus into...

headline: Exxon Saves Billions, But Margins Still Cut In Half
summary: Management once built its transformation story on massive cost discipline — and it's still telling it, louder than ever. Yet as profit margins fall by nearly half, a harder question emerges: if record cost savings can't hold the line, how resilient is the "transformed" company really.

content.title: Exxon Saves Billions, But Margins Still Cut In Half
content.summary: Management once built its transformation story on massive cost discipline — and it's still telling it, louder than ever. Yet as profit margins fall by nearly half, a harder question emerges: if record cost savings can't hold the line, how resilient is the "transformed" company really.

headline: Oil Giant Emerges As An AI-Power Play; Two Key Suppliers Rally
summary: Chevron announced Monday that it will power a huge Microsoft data center in West Texas with natural gas, using gas turbines supplied by GE Vernova and Caterpillar.  GE Vernova stock rose near a buy point, and Caterpillar stock jumped.  Project Kilby, as it is known, is expected to produce 2.7 gigawatts of electricity.

content.title: Oil Giant Emerges As An AI-Power Play; Two Key Suppliers Rally
content.summary: Chevron announced Monday that it will power a huge Microsoft data center in West Texas with natural gas, using gas turbines supplied by GE Vernova and Caterpillar.  GE Vernova stock rose near a buy point, and Caterpillar stock jumped.  Project Kilby, as it is known, is expected to produce 2.7 gigawatts of electricity.
